texto: A secret's only a secret as long as you keep it. Once you tell someone it loses all its power -- for good or ill -- like that, it's just another piece of information. But a real mystery can't be solved, not completely. It's always just out of reach, like a light around the corner; you might catch a glimpse of what it reveals, feel its warmth, but you can't know the heart of it, not really. That's what gives it value: It can't be cracked, it's bigger than you and me, bigger than everything we know.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mark Frost: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “A secret's only a secret as long as you keep it. Once you tell someone it loses all its power -- …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, mystery, secret pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de secret e twin-peaks.

frase_original: A secret's only a secret as long as you keep it. Once you tell someone it loses all its power -- for good or ill -- like that, it's just another piece of information. But a real mystery can't be solved, not completely. It's always just out of reach, like a light around the corner; you might catch a glimpse of what it reveals, feel its warmth, but you can't know the heart of it, not really. That's what gives it value: It can't be cracked, it's bigger than you and me, bigger than everything we know.

texto: Moving forward in time, it is important that we learn to distinguish between mysteries and secrets. Mysteries precede humankind, envelop us and draw us forward into exploration and wonder.
